The challenge for the Board was to manage our risks in a way that ensured the continued delivery of critical and protected clinical services and delivered the key operational objectives set out above. As required by the Scottish Government the Board developed and approved two initial COVID-19 response plans (March 2020) and three separate remobilisation plans (May 2020, July 2020 and February 2021). question: What proportion of the 2024/25 savings were of a recurring nature?
answer: £50.5 million savings were delivered of which £25.2 million are expected to be recurring

The financial plan submitted to Scottish Government in March 2024 projected a £59.1 million deficit for the year after forecasting savings of £34.9 million.
